session.description: The cookie of an active session in FortiAnalyzer.

id.description: An identifier established by the client.

jsonrpc.description: A string specifying the JSON-RPC protocol version.

method.description: A string containing the method name to be invoked.

params.description: A structure that holds the parameter values.

filter.description: Filter expression. 'ackflag', 'assigned', 'mgmt_state', 'triggername', 'eventtype', 'severity', 'eventstatus' and 'tag' are supported. i.e.: ackflag = no and triggername='Local Device Event'. Note: the field 'ackflag' supports = yes/no, !=yes/no and <>yes/no ,  the field 'assigned' only supports = yes/no or !=yes/no, the field 'mgmt_state' only supports (!)= acknowledged/triaged/pending_triage

time-range.description: Time range for source data selection.

end.description: Ending date time. Consider it following the timezone parameter's, ADOM's or FortiAnalyzer's timezone if timezone info is not specified within time range. Format: 'yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZ' (RFC 3339) e.g. '2016-10-17T20:45:37-07:00 or 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss' e.g. '2016-10-17 20:45:37'

start.description: Starting date time. Consider it following the timezone parameter's, ADOM's or FortiAnalyzer's timezone if timezone info is not specified within time range. Format: 'yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZ' (RFC 3339) e.g. '2016-10-17T20:45:37-07:00 or 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss' e.g. '2016-10-17 20:45:37'

timescale.description: Time scale. Only valid for type 'severity-timescale'.

timezone.description: The timezone index or name. Time range in request and date/time if any in response will follow this timezone. See Appendix

filter.description: Filter expression. 'event_value', 'severity', 'trigger_name', 'count', 'comment' and 'flags' are supported. i.e.: trigger_name='Local Device Event' and severity >= 3

end.description: Ending date time. Consider it following the timezone parameter's, ADOM's or FortiAnalyzer's timezone if timezone info is not specified within time range. Format: 'yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZ' (RFC 3339) e.g. '2016-10-17T20:45:37-07:00 or 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss' e.g. '2016-10-17 20:45:37'

start.description: Starting date time. Consider it following the timezone parameter's, ADOM's or FortiAnalyzer's timezone if timezone info is not specified within time range. Format: 'yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssZ' (RFC 3339) e.g. '2016-10-17T20:45:37-07:00 or 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss' e.g. '2016-10-17 20:45:37'

timezone.description: The timezone index or name. Time range in request and date/time if any in response will follow this timezone. See Appendix

mitre-domain.description: Query mitre domain, either enterprise or ics.

option.description: The option of the return data. Metadata is to include the technique name, url and description. Event-count, incident-count and handler-count is to include the statistic of each category.

method.description: A string containing the method name to be invoked.

params.description: A structure that holds the parameter values.

mitre-domain.description: Query mitre domain, either enterprise or ics.

option.description: The option of the return data. Metadata is to include the technique name, url and description. Event-count, incident-count and handler-count is to include the statistic of each category. Event-summary, incident-summary and handler-summary is to include the summary data for the given tehcnique id

end.description: The end time of the query time window.

start.description: The start time of the query time window.

session.description: The cookie of an active session in FortiAnalyzer.

info.description:
Documentation for FortiAnalyzer JSON API

This daemon controls main system functions, including log in and log out. All APIs for this module are under the prefix /sys/.
info.title: FortiAnalyzer JSON API Documentation
